video_title: Top #DDD Videos in Chicago with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Cafe
restaurants.guy_intro: Cafe in Chicago, Illinois. They're going authentic with the sound, the moves, and definitely the flavors of Havana.

dishes.description: Cuban-style chicken stew, seasoned with a rub, seared, then cooked with sofrito, tomato paste, chicken stock, potatoes, and olives, served with yellow rice and sweet plantains.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, outrageous. Now if you grab a piece of sweet plantain and mix it all together, blends it up real nice. Oh, do you know what I do? It seals it. The olive. This takes the cake, man. Olives are the key, though. That's what seals the deal. That is money. Seriously. Tastylicious.

dishes.description: Roasted pork, marinated with a sweet rub made from prunes, bacon, ham, sugar cane, guava, and malta, then slow-roasted for five hours, shredded, and served with white rice, black beans, and plantains.
dishes.guy_response: Really nice dish. Puerco rotizado. It's juicy. I like to eat it with the plantains. Puerco has so many complex flavors. You build flavor upon flavor. I have one more. Tastylicious.

restaurants.name: Bop and Grill
restaurants.guy_intro: So here I am in Chicago to check out a joint where they're putting a little Asian spin on some all-American classics. This is Bop and Grill.

dishes.description: Burger featuring an 80/20 beef patty, topped with a braised mushroom 'truffle duck salad' (made with pork fat, butter, onions, garlic, bonito flakes, anchovy dashi, portobello mushrooms, truffle oil), smoked bacon, Munster cheese, sun-dried tomato confit, and togarashi mayo on a brioche bun.
dishes.guy_response: Dude. Look at that. This burger should definitely deliver the flavors of umami.

restaurants.name: Arazu
restaurants.guy_intro: So here we are in Bucktown in Chicago to check out what may be a Triple D first, a Costa Rican joint. This is Arazu.

dishes.description: A Costa Rican sandwich on fresh French bread, spread with pureed black bean paste, layered with seared ribeye steak, caramelized onions, Munster cheese, and a special Costa Rican lasagna sauce, then pressed.
dishes.guy_response: The beans, prime time. The meat's great. The nice big onions. The lasagna. If you look inside these veins right here, lasagna is running up and down inside of them. I can't stop eating it. The bomb. You rocked it. Flattered. Thank you. Rocked it. Thank you very much. It's super delicious. Flavors just all melt together.

dishes.description: A Costa Rican dish featuring crispy chicharron (pork belly cooked with garlic and salt), served over a base of black bean soup and rice, topped with pico de gallo, avocado, and Costa Rican lasano salsa, served with chips.
dishes.guy_response: The chicharron's on point. Nice fat, it's got a nice crunch. The tomato, the onion, you feel it? I don't need to go anywhere. I'm in Costa Rica right now. I liked it. Great job, man.

restaurants.guy_intro: There's something special about this place. Their burgers are the best. Everything has a unique ingredient on it. It's not your everyday Joe burger. Owner's not your everyday Joe. This is me, Mike Burnett owns a bar. With plenty of ink. Metallica. And burgers named for heavy metal bands.

dishes.description: A 10-ounce burger patty seasoned with salt, pepper, blackening spice, garlic powder, kosher salt, chili powder, paprika, and cayenne, topped with a rich chili (made with ground beef, spices, PBR beer, kidney beans, black beans, tomatoes, hot chili beans, chocolate sauce, and chipotles in adobo), and halijack cheese, served on a pretzel bun with red onion.
dishes.guy_response: That's killer. Well-cooked burger, medium rare. Great chili on it, a little spice. But you know what really enhances this is that pretzel roll. That pretzel roll's legit. That's a wholesome, all-American, great burger. That's up there in the top five burgers on Triple D. It's delicious.

restaurants.name: Paradise Pub
restaurants.guy_intro: But knowing the menu by heart just isn't that unusual. At Paradise Pub. Where fast food is home cooking. When they leave here, I want people to feel like they've been to someone's backyard barbecue. A backyard barbecue run by George and Tony Manos, who started the joint 24 years ago.

dishes.description: A third-pound, fresh, never-frozen char-grilled beef patty, topped with molten Wisconsin Mercs cheddar cheese, pickles, lettuce, tomato, and molten hot onions, served on a grilled twist bun.
dishes.guy_response: Oh. That is juicy. Oh. You guys have a shower? We'll hose you down in the back. It's a really strong cheddar, and it's really strong beef, and it's just on top of that. I taste a nice, great char taste. Cheese is great.

dishes.description: Hand-cut Idaho russet potatoes, aged for three to four months, blanched in beef fat, then finished in beef fat until golden brown, seasoned with salt, pepper, and Parmesan cheese, and served with a homemade truffle aioli (made with egg yolks, lemon juice, virgin olive oil, and white truffle oil).
dishes.guy_response: Crunchy, exterior, tender. Such a richness. More importantly to me than anything, skin on. See, I like pepper on my fries, too. The aioli, nice. It melts right into the fries. I mean, real deal, man. Real deal. Excellent. Truffle fry up. Great fries.
